title: ATX-1 Technique Definition
description: JSON Schema for ATX-1 v2.3 (AEGIS Threat Matrix) technique definitions. Each technique represents a specific failure mechanism in which an AI agent acts outside its governance boundaries. v2.3 removes the severity field in alignment with MITRE ATT&CK and ATLAS conventions, which leave contextual scoring to the consumer.

id.description: Unique technique or sub-technique identifier. Techniques use format T#### or T##### (e.g., T1001, T10001). Sub-techniques use format T####.### or T#####.### (e.g., T9002.001, T10004.010).

name.description: Human-readable technique name in verb-object format

description.description: Full description of the technique and how it manifests

root_cause.description: Structural root cause(s) from RC1-RC4 that enable this technique

agents_of_chaos_case.description: Case study numbers from the Agents of Chaos research (Shapira et al., 2026)

owasp_mapping.description: OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications category mappings (e.g., LLM01, LLM06)

constitutional_article.description: AEGIS constitutional article that addresses this technique

agp_mechanism.description: AEGIS Governance Protocol mechanism(s) that implement the mitigation

mechanism.description: Description of how the mitigation works

v1_id.description: The v1.0 technique ID this technique maps from (null for new techniques in v2.0)

parent_technique.description: For sub-techniques only: the parent technique ID this sub-technique refines (e.g., T10004 for T10004.001).

sub_techniques.description: For parent techniques only: list of sub-technique IDs that refine this technique.
